titleStmt.title: 380. Intelligence Memorandum Prepared by the Central Intelligence Agency

div.head: 380. Intelligence Memorandum Prepared by the Central Intelligence AgencySource: Johnson Library, National Security File, Country File, Laos, Vol. XVIII, Memos, 1/68–1/69. Secret; No Foreign Dissem. A note on the source text indicates that this memorandum was produced by the CIA’s Office of Current Intelligence and coordinated with the Office of National Estimates and the Clandestine Services.
div.p: DEVELOPMENTS IN LAOS DURING THE SUMMER 1968

The rainy season this summer in Laos brought no major or important military changes. The lull in the fighting and the apparent reluctance of the Communists to press their dry season advantage has given the government side a much needed respite. But although the modest gains
made by government troops since June have improved their tactical situation and their morale, the Communists are in a stronger position in Laos than they have been at any time since 1963.
[Here follows the text of the 8-page memorandum.]
